history.content:
<thought>
I am facing East. The cell directly ahead is safe floor, and the goal is farther to the east in the same corridor. Moving forward brings me closer to the goal without risking lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am still facing East. The cell directly ahead is lava, so moving forward would be fatal. I should turn to avoid it and look for the safe floor cell available by changing direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
Facing North, the cell ahead is floor, while the surrounding corridor is still bounded by walls. Moving forward is safe and continues exploration toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing North, and the cell ahead is a safe floor tile. Since there’s no goal visible yet and no immediate hazard in front of me, the best next step is to continue forward to explore the corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation is unchanged and I still face North with a safe floor cell ahead. Continuing forward is the only sensible progress action.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m still facing North in the same corridor-like local view, and the tile directly ahead remains safe. I should keep moving forward to explore and eventually find the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The local observation still shows a safe open cell directly ahead, so advancing is the best available move.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation is unchanged and the cell ahead is still safe, so I will continue forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The view is unchanged again, and moving forward remains safe from the visible local map, so I will keep advancing.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I still only see open floor directly ahead, so the safest progress is to move forward again.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The local view is unchanged, with open floor in front and walls on the sides, so continuing forward is the best safe move.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation is still the same and no hazards are visible ahead, so I will continue forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The view remains unchanged again, so the safest progress is still to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing north, and the cell directly ahead appears blocked by the wall row in front of me, so moving forward is not safe. I should turn to look for an open path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Facing east, the cell directly ahead is lava, so I must not move forward. The goal is visible to the east but there is deadly lava between me and it. To avoid stepping into lava, I should reorient and search for a safer route.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>
